---
title: "README_Project2"
output: html_document
---

Project 2
Repository structure
Background/ – project description / references
Code/ – analysis scripts
DataRaw/ – raw data folder
DataProcessed/ – cleaned/derived datasets
Reports/ – knitted outputs / writeups
Code files created:
Code/Power_Analysis.Rmd – power analysis script for Aims 1 and 2, including table and figure generation
Code/Power_Analysis.html – rendered output of the power analysis script
Reports/Bios_6624_Project2.pdf – final Project 2 report
Reports/Table_1_Aim1_power_thresholds.png – exported image of Table 1
Reports/Table_2_Aim2_alpha_sensitivity.png – exported image of Table 2
Reports/Supplemental_Table_1_Aim1_corr_power.png – exported image of Supplemental Table 1
Reports/Supplemental_Table_2_Full_Aim2_power_results.png – exported image of Supplemental Table 2
Data files used
DataRaw/PrelimData.csv – raw preliminary dataset used to obtain pilot correlations for the power calculations

Completed the following components of the Project 2 power analysis:

Loaded the raw preliminary dataset:
DataRaw/PrelimData.csv
Computed pilot correlations from the preliminary data:
CVLT_CNG3 with IL_6
CVLT_CNG3 with MCP_1
CORT_CNG3 with IL_6
CORT_CNG3 with MCP_1
Calculated Aim 1 omnibus power quantities from preliminary two-predictor models:
fit linear models for CVLT_CNG3 ~ IL_6 + MCP_1
fit linear models for CORT_CNG3 ~ IL_6 + MCP_1
extracted pilot R^2 values
evaluated power at N = 175
calculated required sample size for 80% power using mlrF.overall()
Performed Aim 1 correlation-based power calculations using corr.1samp():
used representative pilot correlations from the observed preliminary range
evaluated power at N = 175
calculated required sample size for 80% power
repeated calculations under three type I error strategies:
unadjusted alpha = 0.05
stricter alpha = 0.01
Bonferroni-adjusted alpha across 48 Aim 1 tests
Created Aim 1 outputs:
Supplemental Table 1: combined correlation-based power calculations
Table 1: minimum representative effect size achieving at least 80% power at N = 175
Figure 1: power for representative Aim 1 effect sizes under alternative type I error strategies
Performed Aim 2 power calculations using corr.2samp() as a simplified approximation to the planned amyloid-by-inflammation interaction analysis:
compared predictor-outcome correlations between amyloid-positive and amyloid-negative groups
varied amyloid-positive : amyloid-negative sample splits:
50:50
60:40
70:30
varied baseline amyloid-negative correlations:
-0.15
-0.25
-0.35
-0.45
varied between-group differences in correlations:
0.10
0.20
0.30
0.40
evaluated power at N = 175
calculated total sample size required for 80% power
repeated calculations under three type I error strategies:
unadjusted alpha = 0.05
stricter alpha = 0.01
Bonferroni-adjusted alpha across 24 Aim 2 tests
Created Aim 2 outputs:
Table 2: alpha sensitivity under a representative scenario
Supplemental Table 2: full Aim 2 power results across planning scenarios
Figure 2: power to detect differences between amyloid groups
Exported report-ready table images to the Reports/ folder using gtsave()
